title: Polyalthia mariannae
taxonomy: Polyalthia mariannae is a member of the family Annonaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. The genus Polyalthia is a genus of trees and shrubs, native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. It is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms, which are divided into numerous families and genera based on their characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Polyalthia mariannae specifically is a species of flowering plant that is native to the island of Guam, where it is considered an endangered species. The classification of Polyalthia mariannae reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the larger family of Annonaceae.
uses: Polyalthia mariannae is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. It is traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Additionally, some studies suggest that Polyalthia mariannae may have antiviral and antibacterial properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Further investigation into the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications is necessary to fully understand its benefits.

preparations: Polyalthia mariannae herbal preparations are primarily used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
